revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 楡山神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=深谷市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[深谷市]]原郷336 |位置 = {{Coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[伊邪那美命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[県社]] |創建 = 伝・[[孝昭天皇]]御代 |例祭 = 10月20日 |地図={{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=楡山神社|coordinates= {{coord2|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=20…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 楡山神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=深谷市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[深谷市]]原郷336 |位置 = {{Coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[伊邪那美命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[県社]] |創建 = 伝・[[孝昭天皇]]御代 |例祭 = 10月20日 |地図={{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=楡山神社|coordinates= {{coord2|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}} }} '''楡山神社'''（にれやまじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[深谷市]]原郷にある[[神社]]。
== 概要 == JR[[深谷駅]]や深谷市役所の北東、[[福川 (埼玉県)|福川]]にかかる[[埼玉県道127号深谷飯塚線|県道127号]]の橋「権現堂(ごんげんどう)橋」の南側に鎮座している<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - [[コトバンク]]</ref>。
創建は[[孝昭天皇]]御代と伝承される<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - 楡山神社</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - 埼玉県神社庁</ref>。地域一帯は[[木の本古墳群]]が広がっており、楡山神社も同名の[[武蔵国の式内社一覧|式内社]]に比定されているため、いずれにしろ歴史は相当古く、その起源は古代に遡る<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。
社名の由来は、神域に[[楡]](ニレ)の木が多かったことによる<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。
江戸時代には[[天台宗]]の東学院が[[別当寺]]となり、「熊野三社大権現」「熊野社」とも呼ばれるようになったが、地元民は相変わらず楡山神社と呼んでいたことが『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記されている<ref name="kotobank"/>。
[[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に郷社に指定され、[[大正]]12年（[[1923年]]）に県社に昇格した<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[伊邪那美命]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://nireyama.main.jp/ 楡山神社] - 公式サイト
{{DEFAULTSORT:にれやましんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:深谷市の歴史]] [[Category:深谷市の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称 = 楡山神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=深谷市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[深谷市]]原郷336 |位置 = {{Coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[伊邪那美命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[県社]] |創建 = 伝・[[孝昭天皇]]御代 |例祭 = 10月20日 |地図={{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=楡山神社|coordinates= {{coord2|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}} }} '''楡山神社'''（にれやまじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[深谷市]]原郷にある[[神社]]。

創建は[[孝昭天皇]]御代と伝承される<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - 楡山神社</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - 埼玉県神社庁</ref>。地域一帯は[[木の本古墳群]]が広がっており、楡山神社も同名の[[武蔵国の式内社一覧|式内社]]に比定されているため、いずれにしろ歴史は相当古く、その起源は古代に遡る<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。

[[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に郷社に指定され、[[大正]]12年（[[1923年]]）に県社に昇格した<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。

{{nihongo|'''Nireyama Shrine'''|楡山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nireyama Shrine'''|楡山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nireyama Shrine'''|楡山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nireyama Shrine'''|楡山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nireyama Shrine'''|楡山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nireyama Shrine'''|楡山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nireyama Shrine'''|楡山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nireyama Shrine'''|楡山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nireyama Shrine'''|楡山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nireyama Shrine'''|楡山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nireyama Shrine'''|楡山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nireyama Shrine'''|楡山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nireyama Shrine'''|楡山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nireyama Shrine'''|楡山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nireyama Shrine'''|楡山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nireyama Shrine'''|楡山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nireyama Shrine'''|楡山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nireyama Shrine'''|楡山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

revision.comment: 2 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 楡山神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=深谷市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[深谷市]]原郷336 |位置 = {{Coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[伊邪那美命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[県社]] |創建 = 伝・[[孝昭天皇]]御代 |例祭 = 10月20日 |地図={{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=楡山神社|coordinates= {{coord2|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}} }} '''楡山神社'''（にれやまじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[深谷市]]原郷にある[[神社]]。
== 概要 == JR[[深谷駅]]や深谷市役所の北東、[[福川 (埼玉県)|福川]]にかかる[[埼玉県道127号深谷飯塚線|県道127号]]の橋「権現堂(ごんげんどう)橋」の南側に鎮座している<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - [[コトバンク]]</ref>。
創建は[[孝昭天皇]]御代と伝承される<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - 楡山神社</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - 埼玉県神社庁</ref>。地域一帯は[[木の本古墳群]]が広がっており、楡山神社も同名の[[武蔵国の式内社一覧|式内社]]に比定されているため、いずれにしろ歴史は相当古く、その起源は古代に遡る<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。
社名の由来は、神域に[[楡]](ニレ)の木が多かったことによる<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。
江戸時代には[[天台宗]]の東学院が[[別当寺]]となり、「熊野三社大権現」「熊野社」とも呼ばれるようになったが、地元民は相変わらず楡山神社と呼んでいたことが『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記されている<ref name="kotobank"/>。
[[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に郷社に指定され、[[大正]]12年（[[1923年]]）に県社に昇格した<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[伊邪那美命]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://nireyama.main.jp/ 楡山神社] - 公式サイト
{{DEFAULTSORT:にれやましんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:深谷市の歴史]] [[Category:深谷市の建築物]]

{{wikidata link|Q134887071}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|楡山神社|version=107007340|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 楡山神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=深谷市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[深谷市]]原郷336 |位置 = {{Coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[伊邪那美命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[県社]] |創建 = 伝・[[孝昭天皇]]御代 |例祭 = 10月20日 |地図={{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=楡山神社|coordinates= {{coord2|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}} }} '''楡山神社'''（にれやまじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[深谷市]]原郷にある[[神社]]。
== 概要 == JR[[深谷駅]]や深谷市役所の北東、[[福川 (埼玉県)|福川]]にかかる[[埼玉県道127号深谷飯塚線|県道127号]]の橋「権現堂(ごんげんどう)橋」の南側に鎮座している<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - [[コトバンク]]</ref>。
創建は[[孝昭天皇]]御代と伝承される<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - 楡山神社</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - 埼玉県神社庁</ref>。地域一帯は[[木の本古墳群]]が広がっており、楡山神社も同名の[[武蔵国の式内社一覧|式内社]]に比定されているため、いずれにしろ歴史は相当古く、その起源は古代に遡る<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。
社名の由来は、神域に[[楡]](ニレ)の木が多かったことによる<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。
江戸時代には[[天台宗]]の東学院が[[別当寺]]となり、「熊野三社大権現」「熊野社」とも呼ばれるようになったが、地元民は相変わらず楡山神社と呼んでいたことが『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記されている<ref name="kotobank"/>。
[[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に郷社に指定され、[[大正]]12年（[[1923年]]）に県社に昇格した<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[伊邪那美命]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://nireyama.main.jp/ 楡山神社] - 公式サイト
{{DEFAULTSORT:にれやましんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:深谷市の歴史]] [[Category:深谷市の建築物]]
==End Japanese==

{{wikidata link|Q134887071}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|楡山神社|version=107007340|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 楡山神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=深谷市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[深谷市]]原郷336 |位置 = {{Coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[伊邪那美命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[県社]] |創建 = 伝・[[孝昭天皇]]御代 |例祭 = 10月20日 |地図={{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=楡山神社|coordinates= {{coord2|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}} }} '''楡山神社'''（にれやまじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[深谷市]]原郷にある[[神社]]。
== 概要 == JR[[深谷駅]]や深谷市役所の北東、[[福川 (埼玉県)|福川]]にかかる[[埼玉県道127号深谷飯塚線|県道127号]]の橋「権現堂(ごんげんどう)橋」の南側に鎮座している<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - [[コトバンク]]</ref>。
創建は[[孝昭天皇]]御代と伝承される<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - 楡山神社</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - 埼玉県神社庁</ref>。地域一帯は[[木の本古墳群]]が広がっており、楡山神社も同名の[[武蔵国の式内社一覧|式内社]]に比定されているため、いずれにしろ歴史は相当古く、その起源は古代に遡る<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。
社名の由来は、神域に[[楡]](ニレ)の木が多かったことによる<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。
江戸時代には[[天台宗]]の東学院が[[別当寺]]となり、「熊野三社大権現」「熊野社」とも呼ばれるようになったが、地元民は相変わらず楡山神社と呼んでいたことが『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記されている<ref name="kotobank"/>。
[[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に郷社に指定され、[[大正]]12年（[[1923年]]）に県社に昇格した<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。
== 祭神 == * [[伊邪那美命]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://nireyama.main.jp/ 楡山神社] - 公式サイト
==End Japanese== [[Category:埼玉県の旧県社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:深谷市の歴史]] [[Category:深谷市の建築物]]

== Japanese Wikipedia content ==
{{神社
|名称 = 楡山神社
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=深谷市}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|深谷市|Q734532}}原郷336
|位置 = {{Coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|display=inline,title}}
|祭神 = {{jalink|伊邪那美命}}
|社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|県社}}
|創建 = 伝・{{jalink|孝昭天皇|Q312841}}御代
|例祭 = 10月20日
|地図={{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=楡山神社|coordinates=
{{coord2|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}}
}}
'''楡山神社'''（にれやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|深谷市|Q734532}}原郷にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

== 概要 ==
JR{{jalink|深谷駅|Q844895}}や深谷市役所の北東、{{jalink|福川 (埼玉県)|Q21654416|福川}}にかかる{{jalink|埼玉県道127号深谷飯塚線|Q11426833|県道127号}}の橋「権現堂(ごんげんどう)橋」の南側に鎮座している<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - {{jalink|コトバンク|Q11302260}}</ref>。

創建は{{jalink|孝昭天皇|Q312841}}御代と伝承される<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - 楡山神社</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - 埼玉県神社庁</ref>。地域一帯は{{jalink|木の本古墳群|Q17211800}}が広がっており、楡山神社も同名の{{jalink|武蔵国の式内社一覧|Q11546334|式内社}}に比定されているため、いずれにしろ歴史は相当古く、その起源は古代に遡る<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。

社名の由来は、神域に{{jalink|楡}}(ニレ)の木が多かったことによる<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。

江戸時代には{{jalink|天台宗|Q668188}}の東学院が{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}となり、「熊野三社大権現」「熊野社」とも呼ばれるようになったが、地元民は相変わらず楡山神社と呼んでいたことが『{{jalink|新編武蔵風土記稿|Q11503943}}』に記されている<ref name="kotobank"/>。

{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に郷社に指定され、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}12年（{{jalink|1923年|Q2169}}）に県社に昇格した<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。

== 祭神 ==
* {{jalink|伊邪那美命}}

== 脚注 ==
{{Reflist}}

== 外部リンク ==
* [http://nireyama.main.jp/ 楡山神社] - 公式サイト


==End Japanese==

{{wikidata link|Q134887071}}

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称 = 楡山神社
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=深谷市}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|深谷市|Q734532}}原郷336
|位置 = {{Coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|display=inline,title}}
|祭神 = {{jalink|伊邪那美命}}
|社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|県社}}
|創建 = 伝・{{jalink|孝昭天皇|Q312841}}御代
|例祭 = 10月20日
|地図={{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=楡山神社|coordinates=
{{coord2|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}}
}}
'''楡山神社'''（にれやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|深谷市|Q734532}}原郷にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 概要 ===
JR{{jalink|深谷駅|Q844895}}や深谷市役所の北東、{{jalink|福川 (埼玉県)|Q21654416|福川}}にかかる{{jalink|埼玉県道127号深谷飯塚線|Q11426833|県道127号}}の橋「権現堂(ごんげんどう)橋」の南側に鎮座している<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - {{jalink|コトバンク|Q11302260}}</ref>。

創建は{{jalink|孝昭天皇|Q312841}}御代と伝承される<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - 楡山神社</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - 埼玉県神社庁</ref>。地域一帯は{{jalink|木の本古墳群|Q17211800}}が広がっており、楡山神社も同名の{{jalink|武蔵国の式内社一覧|Q11546334|式内社}}に比定されているため、いずれにしろ歴史は相当古く、その起源は古代に遡る<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。

社名の由来は、神域に{{jalink|楡}}(ニレ)の木が多かったことによる<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。

江戸時代には{{jalink|天台宗|Q668188}}の東学院が{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}となり、「熊野三社大権現」「熊野社」とも呼ばれるようになったが、地元民は相変わらず楡山神社と呼んでいたことが『{{jalink|新編武蔵風土記稿|Q11503943}}』に記されている<ref name="kotobank"/>。

{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に郷社に指定され、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}12年（{{jalink|1923年|Q2169}}）に県社に昇格した<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|伊邪那美命}}

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 外部リンク ===
* [http://nireyama.main.jp/ 楡山神社] - 公式サイト

{{DEFAULTSORT:にれやましんしや}}

[[JA:楡山神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q134887071}}

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称 = 楡山神社
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=深谷市}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|深谷市|Q734532}}原郷336
|位置 = {{Coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|display=inline,title}}
|祭神 = {{jalink|伊邪那美命}}
|社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|県社}}
|創建 = 伝・{{jalink|孝昭天皇|Q312841}}御代
|例祭 = 10月20日
|地図={{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=楡山神社|coordinates=
{{coord2|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}}
}}
'''楡山神社'''（にれやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|深谷市|Q734532}}原郷にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 概要 ===
JR{{jalink|深谷駅|Q844895}}や深谷市役所の北東、{{jalink|福川 (埼玉県)|Q21654416|福川}}にかかる{{jalink|埼玉県道127号深谷飯塚線|Q11426833|県道127号}}の橋「権現堂(ごんげんどう)橋」の南側に鎮座している<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - {{jalink|コトバンク|Q11302260}}</ref>。

創建は{{jalink|孝昭天皇|Q312841}}御代と伝承される<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - 楡山神社</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - 埼玉県神社庁</ref>。地域一帯は{{jalink|木の本古墳群|Q17211800}}が広がっており、楡山神社も同名の{{jalink|武蔵国の式内社一覧|Q11546334|式内社}}に比定されているため、いずれにしろ歴史は相当古く、その起源は古代に遡る<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。

社名の由来は、神域に{{jalink|楡}}(ニレ)の木が多かったことによる<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。

江戸時代には{{jalink|天台宗|Q668188}}の東学院が{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}となり、「熊野三社大権現」「熊野社」とも呼ばれるようになったが、地元民は相変わらず楡山神社と呼んでいたことが『{{jalink|新編武蔵風土記稿|Q11503943}}』に記されている<ref name="kotobank"/>。

{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に郷社に指定され、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}12年（{{jalink|1923年|Q2169}}）に県社に昇格した<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|伊邪那美命}}

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 外部リンク ===
* [http://nireyama.main.jp/ 楡山神社] - 公式サイト

{{DEFAULTSORT:にれやましんしや}}

[[JA:楡山神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q134887071}}

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称 = 楡山神社
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=深谷市}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|深谷市|Q734532}}原郷336
|位置 = {{Coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|display=inline,title}}
|祭神 = {{jalink|伊邪那美命}}
|社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|県社}}
|創建 = 伝・{{jalink|孝昭天皇|Q312841}}御代
|例祭 = 10月20日
|地図={{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=楡山神社|coordinates=
{{coord2|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}}
}}
'''楡山神社'''（にれやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|深谷市|Q734532}}原郷にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 概要 ===
JR{{jalink|深谷駅|Q844895}}や深谷市役所の北東、{{jalink|福川 (埼玉県)|Q21654416|福川}}にかかる{{jalink|埼玉県道127号深谷飯塚線|Q11426833|県道127号}}の橋「権現堂(ごんげんどう)橋」の南側に鎮座している<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - {{jalink|コトバンク|Q11302260}}</ref>。

創建は{{jalink|孝昭天皇|Q312841}}御代と伝承される<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - 楡山神社</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - 埼玉県神社庁</ref>。地域一帯は{{jalink|木の本古墳群|Q17211800}}が広がっており、楡山神社も同名の{{jalink|武蔵国の式内社一覧|Q11546334|式内社}}に比定されているため、いずれにしろ歴史は相当古く、その起源は古代に遡る<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。

社名の由来は、神域に{{jalink|楡}}(ニレ)の木が多かったことによる<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。

江戸時代には{{jalink|天台宗|Q668188}}の東学院が{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}となり、「熊野三社大権現」「熊野社」とも呼ばれるようになったが、地元民は相変わらず楡山神社と呼んでいたことが『{{jalink|新編武蔵風土記稿|Q11503943}}』に記されている<ref name="kotobank"/>。

{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に郷社に指定され、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}12年（{{jalink|1923年|Q2169}}）に県社に昇格した<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|伊邪那美命}}

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 外部リンク ===
* [http://nireyama.main.jp/ 楡山神社] - 公式サイト

{{DEFAULTSORT:にれやましんしや}}

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称 = 楡山神社
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=深谷市}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|深谷市|Q734532}}原郷336
|位置 = {{Coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|display=inline,title}}
|祭神 = {{jalink|伊邪那美命}}
|社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|県社}}
|創建 = 伝・{{jalink|孝昭天皇|Q312841}}御代
|例祭 = 10月20日
|地図={{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=楡山神社|coordinates=
{{coord2|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}}
}}
'''楡山神社'''（にれやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|深谷市|Q734532}}原郷にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。

=== 概要 ===
JR{{jalink|深谷駅|Q844895}}や深谷市役所の北東、{{jalink|福川 (埼玉県)|Q21654416|福川}}にかかる{{jalink|埼玉県道127号深谷飯塚線|Q11426833|県道127号}}の橋「権現堂(ごんげんどう)橋」の南側に鎮座している<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - {{jalink|コトバンク|Q11302260}}</ref>。

創建は{{jalink|孝昭天皇|Q312841}}御代と伝承される<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - 楡山神社</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - 埼玉県神社庁</ref>。地域一帯は{{jalink|木の本古墳群|Q17211800}}が広がっており、楡山神社も同名の{{jalink|武蔵国の式内社一覧|Q11546334|式内社}}に比定されているため、いずれにしろ歴史は相当古く、その起源は古代に遡る<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。

社名の由来は、神域に{{jalink|楡}}(ニレ)の木が多かったことによる<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。

江戸時代には{{jalink|天台宗|Q668188}}の東学院が{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}となり、「熊野三社大権現」「熊野社」とも呼ばれるようになったが、地元民は相変わらず楡山神社と呼んでいたことが『{{jalink|新編武蔵風土記稿|Q11503943}}』に記されている<ref name="kotobank"/>。

{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）に郷社に指定され、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}12年（{{jalink|1923年|Q2169}}）に県社に昇格した<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* {{jalink|伊邪那美命}}

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 外部リンク ===
* [http://nireyama.main.jp/ 楡山神社] - 公式サイト

{{DEFAULTSORT:にれやましんしや}}

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称 = Nireyama Shrine
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=Fukaya City}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Fukaya|Q734532}} Haragō 336
|位置 = {{Coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|display=inline,title}}

|社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, former {{jalink|prefectural shrine}}
|創建 = Traditionally, reign of {{jalink|Emperor Kōshō|Q312841}}
|例祭 = October 20
|地図={{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Nireyama Shrine|coordinates=
{{coord2|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}}
}}
'''Nireyama Shrine''' (にれやまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Haragō, {{jalink|Fukaya|Q734532}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}.

=== Overview ===
It is located northeast of JR {{jalink|Fukaya Station|Q844895}} and Fukaya City Hall, on the south side of Gongendō Bridge on {{jalink|Saitama Prefectural Route 127 Fukaya-Iizuka Line|Q11426833|Prefectural Route 127}}, which crosses the {{jalink|Fukukawa (Saitama)|Q21654416|Fukukawa River}}<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - {{jalink|Kotobank|Q11302260}}</ref>.

Its foundation is traditionally said to date to the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Kōshō|Q312841}}<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - Nireyama Shrine</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>. The surrounding area contains the {{jalink|Kinomoto Kofun Cluster|Q17211800}}, and Nireyama Shrine is identified with the same-named {{jalink|Shikinaisha listed in Musashi Province|Q11546334|Shikinaisha}}; in any case, its history is very old and its origin is thought to go back to ancient times<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

The shrine name is said to come from the many {{jalink|elm}} trees (nire) in the sacred precinct<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

In the Edo period, the Tendai temple Tōgakuin became its {{jalink|bettōji|Q11396755}}, and the shrine came to be called "Kumano Sansha Daigongen" and "Kumano-sha," but local people continued to call it Nireyama Shrine, as recorded in the ''{{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}''<ref name="kotobank"/>.

In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was designated a village shrine, and in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 12 ({{jalink|1923|Q2169}}), it was promoted to prefectural shrine<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Izanami-no-Mikoto}}

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://nireyama.main.jp/ Nireyama Shrine] - official website

'''Nireyama Shrine''' (にれやまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Haragō, {{jalink|Fukaya|Q734532}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}.

=== Overview ===
It is located northeast of JR {{jalink|Fukaya Station|Q844895}} and Fukaya City Hall, on the south side of Gongendō Bridge on {{jalink|Saitama Prefectural Route 127 Fukaya-Iizuka Line|Q11426833|Prefectural Route 127}}, which crosses the {{jalink|Fukukawa (Saitama)|Q21654416|Fukukawa River}}<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - {{jalink|Kotobank|Q11302260}}</ref>.

Its foundation is traditionally said to date to the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Kōshō|Q312841}}<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - Nireyama Shrine</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>. The surrounding area contains the {{jalink|Kinomoto Kofun Cluster|Q17211800}}, and Nireyama Shrine is identified with the same-named {{jalink|Shikinaisha listed in Musashi Province|Q11546334|Shikinaisha}}; in any case, its history is very old and its origin is thought to go back to ancient times<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

The shrine name is said to come from the many {{jalink|elm}} trees (nire) in the sacred precinct<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

In the Edo period, the Tendai temple Tōgakuin became its {{jalink|bettōji|Q11396755}}, and the shrine came to be called "Kumano Sansha Daigongen" and "Kumano-sha," but local people continued to call it Nireyama Shrine, as recorded in the ''{{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}''<ref name="kotobank"/>.

In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was designated a village shrine, and in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 12 ({{jalink|1923|Q2169}}), it was promoted to prefectural shrine<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Izanami-no-Mikoto}}

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://nireyama.main.jp/ Nireyama Shrine] - official website

{{DEFAULTSORT:にれやましんしや}}

'''Nireyama Shrine''' (にれやまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Haragō, {{jalink|Fukaya|Q734532}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}.

=== Overview ===
It is located northeast of JR {{jalink|Fukaya Station|Q844895}} and Fukaya City Hall, on the south side of Gongendō Bridge on {{jalink|Saitama Prefectural Route 127 Fukaya-Iizuka Line|Q11426833|Prefectural Route 127}}, which crosses the {{jalink|Fukukawa (Saitama)|Q21654416|Fukukawa River}}<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - {{jalink|Kotobank|Q11302260}}</ref>.

Its foundation is traditionally said to date to the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Kōshō|Q312841}}<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - Nireyama Shrine</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>. The surrounding area contains the {{jalink|Kinomoto Kofun Cluster|Q17211800}}, and Nireyama Shrine is identified with the same-named {{jalink|Shikinaisha listed in Musashi Province|Q11546334|Shikinaisha}}; in any case, its history is very old and its origin is thought to go back to ancient times<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

The shrine name is said to come from the many {{jalink|elm}} trees (nire) in the sacred precinct<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

In the Edo period, the Tendai temple Tōgakuin became its {{jalink|bettōji|Q11396755}}, and the shrine came to be called "Kumano Sansha Daigongen" and "Kumano-sha," but local people continued to call it Nireyama Shrine, as recorded in the ''{{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}''<ref name="kotobank"/>.

In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was designated a village shrine, and in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 12 ({{jalink|1923|Q2169}}), it was promoted to prefectural shrine<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Izanami-no-Mikoto}}

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://nireyama.main.jp/ Nireyama Shrine] - official website

{{DEFAULTSORT:にれやましんしや}}

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称 = Nireyama Shrine

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}}
}}
'''Nireyama Shrine''' (にれやまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Haragō, {{jalink|Fukaya|Q734532}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}.

=== Overview ===
It is located northeast of JR {{jalink|Fukaya Station|Q844895}} and Fukaya City Hall, on the south side of Gongendō Bridge on {{jalink|Saitama Prefectural Route 127 Fukaya-Iizuka Line|Q11426833|Prefectural Route 127}}, which crosses the {{jalink|Fukukawa (Saitama)|Q21654416|Fukukawa River}}<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - {{jalink|Kotobank|Q11302260}}</ref>.

Its foundation is traditionally said to date to the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Kōshō|Q312841}}<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - Nireyama Shrine</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>. The surrounding area contains the {{jalink|Kinomoto Kofun Cluster|Q17211800}}, and Nireyama Shrine is identified with the same-named {{jalink|Shikinaisha listed in Musashi Province|Q11546334|Shikinaisha}}; in any case, its history is very old and its origin is thought to go back to ancient times<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

The shrine name is said to come from the many {{jalink|elm}} trees (nire) in the sacred precinct<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

In the Edo period, the Tendai temple Tōgakuin became its {{jalink|bettōji|Q11396755}}, and the shrine came to be called "Kumano Sansha Daigongen" and "Kumano-sha," but local people continued to call it Nireyama Shrine, as recorded in the ''{{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}''<ref name="kotobank"/>.

In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was designated a village shrine, and in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 12 ({{jalink|1923|Q2169}}), it was promoted to prefectural shrine<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Izanami-no-Mikoto}}

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://nireyama.main.jp/ Nireyama Shrine] - official website

{{DEFAULTSORT:にれやましんしや}}

== Japanese content ==

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}}
}}
'''Nireyama Shrine''' (にれやまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Haragō, {{jalink|Fukaya|Q734532}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}.

=== Overview ===
It is located northeast of JR {{jalink|Fukaya Station|Q844895}} and Fukaya City Hall, on the south side of Gongendō Bridge on {{jalink|Saitama Prefectural Route 127 Fukaya-Iizuka Line|Q11426833|Prefectural Route 127}}, which crosses the {{jalink|Fukukawa (Saitama)|Q21654416|Fukukawa River}}<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - {{jalink|Kotobank|Q11302260}}</ref>.

Its foundation is traditionally said to date to the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Kōshō|Q312841}}<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - Nireyama Shrine</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>. The surrounding area contains the {{jalink|Kinomoto Kofun Cluster|Q17211800}}, and Nireyama Shrine is identified with the same-named {{jalink|Shikinaisha listed in Musashi Province|Q11546334|Shikinaisha}}; in any case, its history is very old and its origin is thought to go back to ancient times<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

The shrine name is said to come from the many {{jalink|elm}} trees (nire) in the sacred precinct<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

In the Edo period, the Tendai temple Tōgakuin became its {{jalink|bettōji|Q11396755}}, and the shrine came to be called "Kumano Sansha Daigongen" and "Kumano-sha," but local people continued to call it Nireyama Shrine, as recorded in the ''{{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}''<ref name="kotobank"/>.

In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it was designated a village shrine, and in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 12 ({{jalink|1923|Q2169}}), it was promoted to prefectural shrine<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{jalink|Izanami-no-Mikoto}}

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://nireyama.main.jp/ Nireyama Shrine] - official website

{{DEFAULTSORT:にれやましんしや}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}}
}}
'''Nireyama Shrine''' (にれやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Haragō, {{ill|lt=Fukaya|Q734532|WD=Q734532}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}.

=== Overview ===
It is located northeast of JR {{ill|lt=Fukaya Station|Q844895|WD=Q844895}} and Fukaya City Hall, on the south side of Gongendō Bridge on {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefectural Route 127 Fukaya-Iizuka Line|Q11426833|WD=Q11426833|lt=Prefectural Route 127}}, which crosses the {{ill|lt=Fukukawa (Saitama)|Q21654416|WD=Q21654416|lt=Fukukawa River}}<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260}}</ref>.

Its foundation is traditionally said to date to the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841}}<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - Nireyama Shrine</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>. The surrounding area contains the {{ill|lt=Kinomoto Kofun Cluster|Q17211800|WD=Q17211800}}, and Nireyama Shrine is identified with the same-named {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha listed in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|lt=Shikinaisha}}; in any case, its history is very old and its origin is thought to go back to ancient times<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

The shrine name is said to come from the many {{ill||lt=elm|UNKNOWN}} trees (nire) in the sacred precinct<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

In the Edo period, the Tendai temple Tōgakuin became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}, and the shrine came to be called "Kumano Sansha Daigongen" and "Kumano-sha," but local people continued to call it Nireyama Shrine, as recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}''<ref name="kotobank"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was designated a village shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1923|Q2169|WD=Q2169}}), it was promoted to prefectural shrine<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill||lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://nireyama.main.jp/ Nireyama Shrine] - official website

{{DEFAULTSORT:にれやましんしや}}

{{nihongo|'''Nireyama Shrine'''|楡山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称 = Nireyama Shrine
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=Fukaya City}}

{{coord2|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}}
}}
'''Nireyama Shrine''' (にれやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Haragō, {{ill|lt=Fukaya|Q734532|WD=Q734532}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}.

=== Overview ===
It is located northeast of JR {{ill|lt=Fukaya Station|Q844895|WD=Q844895}} and Fukaya City Hall, on the south side of Gongendō Bridge on {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefectural Route 127 Fukaya-Iizuka Line|Q11426833|WD=Q11426833|lt=Prefectural Route 127}}, which crosses the {{ill|lt=Fukukawa (Saitama)|Q21654416|WD=Q21654416|lt=Fukukawa River}}<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260}}</ref>.

Its foundation is traditionally said to date to the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841}}<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - Nireyama Shrine</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>. The surrounding area contains the {{ill|lt=Kinomoto Kofun Cluster|Q17211800|WD=Q17211800}}, and Nireyama Shrine is identified with the same-named {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha listed in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|lt=Shikinaisha}}; in any case, its history is very old and its origin is thought to go back to ancient times<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

The shrine name is said to come from the many {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=elm|1=UNKNOWN}} trees (nire) in the sacred precinct<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

In the Edo period, the Tendai temple Tōgakuin became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}, and the shrine came to be called "Kumano Sansha Daigongen" and "Kumano-sha," but local people continued to call it Nireyama Shrine, as recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}''<ref name="kotobank"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was designated a village shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1923|Q2169|WD=Q2169}}), it was promoted to prefectural shrine<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://nireyama.main.jp/ Nireyama Shrine] - official website

{{DEFAULTSORT:にれやましんしや}}

|例祭 = October 20
|地図={{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Nireyama Shrine|coordinates=
{{coord2|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}}
}}
'''Nireyama Shrine''' (にれやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Haragō, {{ill|lt=Fukaya|Q734532|WD=Q734532}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}.

=== Overview ===
It is located northeast of JR {{ill|lt=Fukaya Station|Q844895|WD=Q844895}} and Fukaya City Hall, on the south side of Gongendō Bridge on {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefectural Route 127 Fukaya-Iizuka Line|Q11426833|WD=Q11426833|lt=Prefectural Route 127}}, which crosses the {{ill|lt=Fukukawa (Saitama)|Q21654416|WD=Q21654416|lt=Fukukawa River}}<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260}}</ref>.

Its foundation is traditionally said to date to the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841}}<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - Nireyama Shrine</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>. The surrounding area contains the {{ill|lt=Kinomoto Kofun Cluster|Q17211800|WD=Q17211800}}, and Nireyama Shrine is identified with the same-named {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha listed in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|lt=Shikinaisha}}; in any case, its history is very old and its origin is thought to go back to ancient times<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

The shrine name is said to come from the many {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=elm|1=UNKNOWN}} trees (nire) in the sacred precinct<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

In the Edo period, the Tendai temple Tōgakuin became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}, and the shrine came to be called "Kumano Sansha Daigongen" and "Kumano-sha," but local people continued to call it Nireyama Shrine, as recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}''<ref name="kotobank"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was designated a village shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1923|Q2169|WD=Q2169}}), it was promoted to prefectural shrine<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q682306}}

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===

The surrounding area contains the {{ill|lt=Kinomoto Kofun Cluster|Q17211800|WD=Q17211800}}, and Nireyama Shrine is identified with the same-named {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha listed in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|lt=Shikinaisha}}; in any case, its history is very old and its origin is thought to go back to ancient times<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

In the Edo period, the Tendai temple Tōgakuin became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}, and the shrine came to be called "Kumano Sansha Daigongen" and "Kumano-sha," but local people continued to call it Nireyama Shrine, as recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}''<ref name="kotobank"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was designated a village shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1923|Q2169|WD=Q2169}}), it was promoted to prefectural shrine<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q682306}}

=== Notes ===

'''Nireyama Shrine''' (にれやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Haragō, {{ill|lt=Fukaya|Q734532|WD=Q734532}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}.

=== Overview ===
It is located northeast of JR {{ill|lt=Fukaya Station|Q844895|WD=Q844895}} and Fukaya City Hall, on the south side of Gongendō Bridge on {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefectural Route 127 Fukaya-Iizuka Line|Q11426833|WD=Q11426833|lt=Prefectural Route 127}}, which crosses the {{ill|lt=Fukukawa (Saitama)|Q21654416|WD=Q21654416|lt=Fukukawa River}}<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260}}</ref>.

Its foundation is traditionally said to date to the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841}}<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - Nireyama Shrine</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>. The surrounding area contains the {{ill|lt=Kinomoto Kofun Cluster|Q17211800|WD=Q17211800}}, and Nireyama Shrine is identified with the same-named {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha listed in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|lt=Shikinaisha}}; in any case, its history is very old and its origin is thought to go back to ancient times<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

The shrine name is said to come from the many {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=elm|1=UNKNOWN}} trees (nire) in the sacred precinct<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

In the Edo period, the Tendai temple Tōgakuin became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}, and the shrine came to be called "Kumano Sansha Daigongen" and "Kumano-sha," but local people continued to call it Nireyama Shrine, as recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}''<ref name="kotobank"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it was designated a village shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1923|Q2169|WD=Q2169}}), it was promoted to prefectural shrine<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q682306}}

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}}
}}
'''Nireyama Shrine''' (にれやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Haragō, {{ill|lt=Fukaya|Q734532|WD=Q734532|1=Fukaya, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== Overview ===
It is located northeast of JR {{ill|lt=Fukaya Station|Q844895|WD=Q844895|1=Fukaya Station}} and Fukaya City Hall, on the south side of Gongendō Bridge on {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefectural Route 127 Fukaya-Iizuka Line|Q11426833|WD=Q11426833|lt=Prefectural Route 127|1=Saitama Prefectural Road Route 127}}, which crosses the {{ill|lt=Fukukawa (Saitama)|Q21654416|WD=Q21654416|lt=Fukukawa River|1=Fuku River}}<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260|1=Kotobank}}</ref>.

Its foundation is traditionally said to date to the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - Nireyama Shrine</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>. The surrounding area contains the {{ill|lt=Kinomoto Kofun Cluster|Q17211800|WD=Q17211800|1=Kinomoto Kofun Cluster}}, and Nireyama Shrine is identified with the same-named {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha listed in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|lt=Shikinaisha|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}}; in any case, its history is very old and its origin is thought to go back to ancient times<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

The shrine name is said to come from the many {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=elm|1=UNKNOWN}} trees (nire) in the sacred precinct<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

In the Edo period, the Tendai temple Tōgakuin became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}, and the shrine came to be called "Kumano Sansha Daigongen" and "Kumano-sha," but local people continued to call it Nireyama Shrine, as recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}''<ref name="kotobank"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was designated a village shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1923|Q2169|WD=Q2169|1=1923}}), it was promoted to prefectural shrine<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://nireyama.main.jp/ Nireyama Shrine] - official website

{{DEFAULTSORT:にれやましんしや}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}}
}}
'''Nireyama Shrine''' (にれやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Haragō, {{ill|lt=Fukaya|Q734532|WD=Q734532|1=Fukaya, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== Overview ===
It is located northeast of JR {{ill|lt=Fukaya Station|Q844895|WD=Q844895|1=Fukaya Station}} and Fukaya City Hall, on the south side of Gongendō Bridge on {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefectural Route 127 Fukaya-Iizuka Line|Q11426833|WD=Q11426833|lt=Prefectural Route 127|1=Saitama Prefectural Road Route 127}}, which crosses the {{ill|lt=Fukukawa (Saitama)|Q21654416|WD=Q21654416|lt=Fukukawa River|1=Fuku River}}<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260|1=Kotobank}}</ref>.

Its foundation is traditionally said to date to the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - Nireyama Shrine</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>. The surrounding area contains the {{ill|lt=Kinomoto Kofun Cluster|Q17211800|WD=Q17211800|1=Kinomoto Kofun Cluster}}, and Nireyama Shrine is identified with the same-named {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha listed in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|lt=Shikinaisha|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}}; in any case, its history is very old and its origin is thought to go back to ancient times<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

The shrine name is said to come from the many {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=elm|1=UNKNOWN}} trees (nire) in the sacred precinct<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

In the Edo period, the Tendai temple Tōgakuin became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}, and the shrine came to be called "Kumano Sansha Daigongen" and "Kumano-sha," but local people continued to call it Nireyama Shrine, as recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}''<ref name="kotobank"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was designated a village shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1923|Q2169|WD=Q2169|1=1923}}), it was promoted to prefectural shrine<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://nireyama.main.jp/ Nireyama Shrine] - official website

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称 = Nireyama Shrine

|所在地 = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Fukaya|Q734532|WD=Q734532|1=Fukaya, Saitama}} Haragō 336

|例祭 = October 20
|地図={{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Nireyama Shrine|coordinates=
{{coord2|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}}
}}
'''Nireyama Shrine''' (にれやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Haragō, {{ill|lt=Fukaya|Q734532|WD=Q734532|1=Fukaya, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== Overview ===
It is located northeast of JR {{ill|lt=Fukaya Station|Q844895|WD=Q844895|1=Fukaya Station}} and Fukaya City Hall, on the south side of Gongendō Bridge on {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefectural Route 127 Fukaya-Iizuka Line|Q11426833|WD=Q11426833|lt=Prefectural Route 127|1=Saitama Prefectural Road Route 127}}, which crosses the {{ill|lt=Fukukawa (Saitama)|Q21654416|WD=Q21654416|lt=Fukukawa River|1=Fuku River}}<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260|1=Kotobank}}</ref>.

Its foundation is traditionally said to date to the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - Nireyama Shrine</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>. The surrounding area contains the {{ill|lt=Kinomoto Kofun Cluster|Q17211800|WD=Q17211800|1=Kinomoto Kofun Cluster}}, and Nireyama Shrine is identified with the same-named {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha listed in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|lt=Shikinaisha|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}}; in any case, its history is very old and its origin is thought to go back to ancient times<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

The shrine name is said to come from the many {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=elm|1=UNKNOWN}} trees (nire) in the sacred precinct<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

In the Edo period, the Tendai temple Tōgakuin became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}, and the shrine came to be called "Kumano Sansha Daigongen" and "Kumano-sha," but local people continued to call it Nireyama Shrine, as recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}''<ref name="kotobank"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was designated a village shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1923|Q2169|WD=Q2169|1=1923}}), it was promoted to prefectural shrine<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}

The shrine name is said to come from the many {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=elm|1=UNKNOWN}} trees (nire) in the sacred precinct<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

In the Edo period, the Tendai temple Tōgakuin became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}, and the shrine came to be called "Kumano Sansha Daigongen" and "Kumano-sha," but local people continued to call it Nireyama Shrine, as recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}''<ref name="kotobank"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was designated a village shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1923|Q2169|WD=Q2169|1=1923}}), it was promoted to prefectural shrine<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

|例祭 = October 20

{{coord2|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|12|15.5|N|139|17|55.4|E}}}}
}}
'''Nireyama Shrine''' (にれやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Haragō, {{ill|lt=Fukaya|Q734532|WD=Q734532|1=Fukaya, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== Overview ===
It is located northeast of JR {{ill|lt=Fukaya Station|Q844895|WD=Q844895|1=Fukaya Station}} and Fukaya City Hall, on the south side of Gongendō Bridge on {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefectural Route 127 Fukaya-Iizuka Line|Q11426833|WD=Q11426833|lt=Prefectural Route 127|1=Saitama Prefectural Road Route 127}}, which crosses the {{ill|lt=Fukukawa (Saitama)|Q21654416|WD=Q21654416|lt=Fukukawa River|1=Fuku River}}<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260|1=Kotobank}}</ref>.

Its foundation is traditionally said to date to the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - Nireyama Shrine</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>. The surrounding area contains the {{ill|lt=Kinomoto Kofun Cluster|Q17211800|WD=Q17211800|1=Kinomoto Kofun Cluster}}, and Nireyama Shrine is identified with the same-named {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha listed in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|lt=Shikinaisha|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}}; in any case, its history is very old and its origin is thought to go back to ancient times<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

The shrine name is said to come from the many {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=elm|1=UNKNOWN}} trees (nire) in the sacred precinct<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

In the Edo period, the Tendai temple Tōgakuin became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}, and the shrine came to be called "Kumano Sansha Daigongen" and "Kumano-sha," but local people continued to call it Nireyama Shrine, as recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}''<ref name="kotobank"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was designated a village shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1923|Q2169|WD=Q2169|1=1923}}), it was promoted to prefectural shrine<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}

=== Notes ===

'''Nireyama Shrine''' (にれやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Haragō, {{ill|lt=Fukaya|Q734532|WD=Q734532|1=Fukaya, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.

=== Overview ===
It is located northeast of JR {{ill|lt=Fukaya Station|Q844895|WD=Q844895|1=Fukaya Station}} and Fukaya City Hall, on the south side of Gongendō Bridge on {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefectural Route 127 Fukaya-Iizuka Line|Q11426833|WD=Q11426833|lt=Prefectural Route 127|1=Saitama Prefectural Road Route 127}}, which crosses the {{ill|lt=Fukukawa (Saitama)|Q21654416|WD=Q21654416|lt=Fukukawa River|1=Fuku River}}<ref name="kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/楡山神社-3038796 楡山神社とは] - {{ill|lt=Kotobank|Q11302260|WD=Q11302260|1=Kotobank}}</ref>.

Its foundation is traditionally said to date to the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}<ref name="yuisho">[http://nireyama.main.jp/nire/ 由緒] - Nireyama Shrine</ref><ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/9944 楡山神社] - Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>. The surrounding area contains the {{ill|lt=Kinomoto Kofun Cluster|Q17211800|WD=Q17211800|1=Kinomoto Kofun Cluster}}, and Nireyama Shrine is identified with the same-named {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha listed in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|lt=Shikinaisha|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}}; in any case, its history is very old and its origin is thought to go back to ancient times<ref name="kotobank"/><ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

The shrine name is said to come from the many {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=elm|1=UNKNOWN}} trees (nire) in the sacred precinct<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

In the Edo period, the Tendai temple Tōgakuin became its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}, and the shrine came to be called "Kumano Sansha Daigongen" and "Kumano-sha," but local people continued to call it Nireyama Shrine, as recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}''<ref name="kotobank"/>.

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was designated a village shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1923|Q2169|WD=Q2169|1=1923}}), it was promoted to prefectural shrine<ref name="yuisho"/><ref name="jinjacho"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
